verdict_reasoning: GCGR is a class B GPCR (seven-pass transmembrane receptor) with extracellular N-terminal domain and extracellular loops. As a member of the secretin receptor family, it is a canonical surface-anchored receptor whose ligand (glucagon hormone) engages its extracellularly-exposed binding surface. Binders including antibodies, small molecules, and peptide agonists/antagonists access the receptor from the extracellular face.
